name: Ashford, Olive Irene
motto: A well-filled purse is the gift of our nurse.
clubs: Chairman Missionary Work of Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: Olive never could see how some people get their lessons so quickly but she always gets there after much worry. Doctor Ashford is always willing to minister to the sick. Her remedies are endless, so is her popcorn. She has Ballard Hall smelling like a popcorn stand all winter. It must be these traits which make her such a famous basketball guard. Olive is an enthusiastic Y. W. C. A. worker. She is one of the kind you do not hear so much about but one who does the work due to her generous spirit and untiring efforts to be a help to others.

name: Atwood, Grace E.
motto: Her words are trusty heralds to her mind.
description: Though Grace came to us from the Bay state, no New Hampshirite could be more loyal to her Alma Mater than she. She has plunged into all her college duties, from her many enterprises in "math" to the responsibility of firm and numerous friendships, with a vim and earnestness which cause us all to admire her. In spite of her quiet dignity many homesick and sorrowful freshmen, most of them young ladies have come to her for consolation. In fact during her own freshman year Grace was forced to spend a good bit of her time in comforting and advising. No activity in which she engages is ever neglected. We are sure that she will agree with us that it is a cause for rejoicing that engineers in the service do not always have to go to France.

name: Baker, Margaret Edna
motto: There's Egypt in her dreamy eyes
description: Margaret is one of those who are continually singing the praises of the North country. She came to us filled with the importance of Solomon's store, but we have recently learned, much to our regret, that the store has burned down. She is one of few who have a sunny disposition and except for her hair-raising, blood-curdling shriek when one of her numerous crushes passes by, she would be a model co-ed. Like many of us, she had ambitions to be a songstress but she found that the Glee Club needed no more basses, so has settled down to sharking Laird's Bacteriology courses.

name: Barker, Forrest Allen
motto: That's an awful way to talk
description: The only mistake Bark ever made was to enter college with the '18 class; he soon realized his mistake however and joined our gang our Sophomore year. The women are one of "Ford's" weaknesses. He takes in all the social functions, every time with a different one, (first one and then the other.) There is safety in numbers but "Ford" argues on the size of the number. You really can't blame him for having a good time 'cause the army doesn't give one much time for social engagements.

name: Bartlett, Edward Emery
motto: If there should be another flood, For refuge hither fly; For 'tho the world should be submerged, This book would still be dry
description: After glancing at this fellow's map, we are inclined to believe that he finds the above statement true. He has the appearance of a slip stick fiend, and a juggler of electric sparks, rather than possessing the usual placidly studious, and empty expression of most Electrical Engineers. Besides he specializes in Newmarket and helps to manage the cross country team, so we must admit that beauty is only on the surface.

name: Belyea, Clement Chipman
description: Like many another sentimental chap whom the delusive fragrance of H2S has lured within the fateful precincts of Conant Hall, this youthful giant is still heroically struggling amid broken glass, visits to the Dean, and differential calculus, with structural formulas somewhat at fault. Although library courses have a strong attraction for him he never finds much time for graft courses and society, but prefers such diversions as higher mathematics. Of this branch of his work Clement is so particularly fond that nearly every math. course offered to Chemickers he has taken at least twice. If you ever care to hear a complete and vivid portrayal of a man's character, (such as may not be found in his autobiography) ask Bell about Garry.

name: Benson, Priscilla
motto: A friend to all who know her
description: Priscilla never had to fear the stern eye of the matron or inspection day for her rooms were always in apple-pie order, but unfortunately she could not keep the equations in Chemistry in as good order and wondered what Chem. 51 had to do with housekeeping for two, anyway. Until this fall we could not understand the attractions of Lebanon but have since been told it is "Elmer." Whatever you do, Priscilla, don't forget the bunch on the third floor.

name: Bradley, Josephine
motto: One look in 'Jo's' eyes makes one think of deep, placid lakes, of distant mountain scenes and her personality has the charm of a twilight after sunset.
description: This serene and patriotic young miss, transferred to New Hampshire College from Radcliffe, and the faculty granted her the privilege of joining a regular class, with which we hope she will graduate. But honestly, Jo, why did you change?

name: Brown, Charles Edward
motto: A dillar, a dollar, a ten o'clock scholar
description: "Brownie" is a versatile guy. He's taken nearly every course in the curriculum except the 2-yr. Aggie. Since that fatal night of the minstrel show he has been hammering the ivories and it is rumored that he has pupils in this gentle art, in the heart of the wicked city. "Brownie" is quite some Beau Brummel in his home town, and also a worldly wise club man. We predict a great future for him in this line of endeavor and no doubt he will some time be grand past master of the ancient and honorable order of hoodwinked owls.

name: Callender, Benjamin Richard
motto: Gee Whiz—yer don't reely mean it!
description: When "Pooch" landed on the plains of Durham from the frozen hills, he resolved to be on the inside looking out on the wicked world. Therefore Piety, Chastity, and Obedience were his watchwords. The sophomore year saw a great change in "Pooch," for behold, the butterfly emerged from the cocoon. He first cast a slur on the fair town of Newmarket after coming back from a visit, frozen. Quoth "Pooch," "nevermore," so now when he wends his way to the deck singing, "O, Little Town of Bethlehem," we know that he bids the sirens hence.

name: Chamberlain, Muriel
motto: A good wit will make use of anything.
description: The phrase is an apt one as applied to Muriel for we defy anyone to mention anything, anywhere or at any time which her ingenuity will not make use of. Strange as it may seem her wit, even when turned in our direction has no sting—for we know that no malice lies behind it—only a spirit of good comradeship and an irrepressible sense of humor. That sense of humor, indeed, even when made manifest merely by the expression of the owner's face is likely to result in the disgrace of all who witness it. Skillful in art, clever in wit, and true in friendship, we're willing to take our hats off to you, Muriel.

name: Center, M. Elizabeth
motto: O sleep it is a gentle thing, beloved from pole to pole.
clubs: Treasurer of Red Cross (Branch of N. H. C.) (3)
description: When Beth isn't cooking or writing themes or attending Red Cross meetings, she always has one occupation left—"I'd rather sleep than anything else when there's nothing else to do." Beth's friends all expect her to be the leading modiste on Fifth Avenue within a few years, for they say if she'd just give her "creations" a French name, they'd bear every mark of being just from Paris. Indeed rumor has it that she can, when so minded, produce a gown that is a few hours ahead of "Gay Paree." Beth doesn't mind their kidding, but though she admits there's a whole psychology to dress, she has ambitions along other lines.

name: Clapp, Arthur Everett
motto: To sit up nights and dance,
description: "Clapper" was an athlete in prep school, but now he trains with Prescott's Oriental Laundry Syndicate, deducting his salary from excess profits and the manager receives a voucher for a share in the Durham Fertilizer Company in return. "Art" will probably receive his sheepskin if Professor Simmers can find out if he attended the Education 4 course, and will be a highly respectable highwayman for tobacco and matches.

name: Cochrane, Thomas Joseph
motto: A little spark may smirk unseen
description: The saying is that "Tommie" was born on the border between Scotland and Ireland. Migrating to this country he decided to become a student of N. H. C. registering in the Chem. course. On arriving in Durham he took up his abode on the outskirts of the city and became proficient in the art of slinging hash at the "Union Goulash Shop." For some unknown reason "Tommie" tried to get under the draft but fell short. As a social lion he has no equal: took a course in Horti. at Ballard Hall and flunked out.

name: Cressey, Mary Robinson
motto: 'Tis beauty that doth oft make woman proud; 'Tis virtue that doth make them most admired; 'Tis government that makes them seem divine.
description: Mary is a commuter, belonging to "that Dover crowd." Her favorite walk on the campus is to and from the railroad station but now and then Grant's apple pie and ice-cream lure her to a trip down town with the rest of the bunch. In spite of taking the Wellesley exams and passing them with flying colors Mary decided to come to N. H. C. Thanks to the turning factor! The only trouble Mary finds with us is that the marks do not run higher than 100 for she would "go over the top" every time. Mary is noted for her dominant courage and persistence. Anything attempted by her is sure to see its finish with the highest degree of perfection. She has "the human will, that force unseen, that can hew its way to any goal."

name: Cullinan, John Francis
motto: What care I how fair she be, if she be not fair to me
description: Jack found a paddle somewhere and came to us from up the "Creek." He had an idea that he was born a Mechanical Engineer, but when Arnie and King James told him he was a chemist he immediately shifted to the Chem. course and has always since deplored the loss of sleep due to working on those descriptive plates. Jack thought Frankie was weak in addition, so he took a couple of overcuts in Math., but was promptly nailed and ever since has had high regard for Frankie's mathematical powers. Jack aspired to be an engineer on a steam roller but it wasn't a success so he decided to become an aviator instead.

name: Davis, Otto Winfred
motto: Where d'you get off with stuff
description: Pa Taylor said that this guy is a little more mature than the rest of us, but that point is still to be proven. "Oswald" got the habit of coming about four weeks late and going a few days early each semester. He shunned high society even to the extent of living in the suburbs of Durham for two years, but finally was persuaded to live in town. As a social star his radiance is somewhat dim, but when it comes to cutting classes for a hike into the North Country he is right there. Since the five-dollar fine rule hit his bank account Dave has been a little more punctual. The chief ambition of this Concordite is to land one of those State job grafts.

name: Dimond, Blanche
motto: I'm thru with all mankind—
description: About every two weeks Blanche never failed to declare quite emphatically that she was thru with men forever but after "Zebe" came she ceased to rave and her grin has become broader, if that is possible. In all classes "Blynche" manages to shine even if she is always "unprepared." As a true friend she can not be beaten and if she takes up all work as enthusiastically as she has here at college, success will be hers.

name: Doherty, Mildred Eva
motto: What is worth doing at all is worth doing well
description: As a freshman we sometimes thought Mildred was a commuter, and then we were informed that she was enjoying the pleasure of home at meal time at Professor Simmers', while we, luckless mortals, were partaking of a strictly dormitory diet. When she emigrated from Pinkerton she brought with her other accomplishments, her ability for basketball which was one of the supporting features of our first 1919 team.

name: Dustin, Cecil Calvert
motto: The pen is mightier than the sword
clubs: Assistant News Editor The New Hampshire (2)
News Editor and Managing Editor The New Hampshire (3)
description: Dignified, conscientious, peaceful Dustin, well, maybe so—and that's the impression that you get from his innocent appearance, but that is not all. He shows great aptitude in judging men, especially of the roughneck variety. Behold his lion-hearted roommate—and ask him who sweeps the floor. "Dusty" is boss of the GRANITE and the New Hampshire, but he gets dusty trying to boss Davis. Unmagnetized by the co-eds, a lover of books, animals and Bot. 55 this gent should become a Doctor of Laws instead of a mere horse doctor.

name: Edwards, Mary Laurene
motto: Laugh and the world laughs with you
description: When Laurene reached Durham she at once enquired when the return train for Franklin left, but as there was none that day, she resigned herself to fate and entered the social whirl with zeal. Billie is one of those adventurous souls who believes in trying anything once from basketball to starring in the minstrel show. The first year her motto was "Variety is the spice of life," and she was seen with many admirers. Then along came a big football man, and since then, her ideas have changed, and she is now waiting for the war to end.

name: Emery, Henry Alfred
description: "Hank" claims that his home is in Auburn, but we can't understand why it is that when he says he is going home he goes to Derry. He has been very successful so far in his college career having managed to stick to the Engineering Course and to steer clear of the co-eds. But if there was tobogganing all the year 'round and if "Hank" ever learned to dance; O Boy! As a substitute for his fraternity brothers who have gone to war, "Hank" has proven himself capable and qualified for any emergency.

name: Emery, Ralph Benton
description: Doc is "Pa" Curtis's right hand man in ruling the destinies of the Dorm. Doc's energies were expended, at one time during his college career, on promoting the growth on his upper lip. Now this is not being done in the best of agricultural circles so he has time to look after the interests of the Aggie Club and manage it to its best advantages. As a student Doc is rated high by the Aggie Profs.

name: Ewart, Raymond James
description: Ray came to New Hampshire from Lawrence, the Mill Town, with the ambition of becoming a gentleman farmer. He entered the two-year course but was persuaded by "Chuck" Weigel to become a real college man and register Four Year. It has been rumored that his bashfulness was the only reason that his attentions did not commute from Dover. He is a very enthusiastic worker in anything that he is interested in as shown by his rapid promotion in the Military Department.

name: Fenderson, Kendrick Elwell
motto: Now ain't that a good 'un
description: Just where he lives is the question; some of the time he is here, some of the time in Dover, but most of the time on the road. The longest time he ever remained in one place was when he was sick. Question number two is, "Which woman is ahead in the mad race for his fair hand?" Ask him about the time he had three girls signed up for the same dance. He has been asked to run a Mildred Champagne column in The New Hampshire entitled, "Advice for the Lovesick." No doubt it will be a great success.

name: Garland, Oscar Leavitt
description: It is claimed that this is the gent that keeps "Nick" Glidden out of jail. If this is true he sure is some lawyer. At any rate he has the distinction of not having his picture in any rogues' gallery in the state. He doesn't look much like a guy that would start a rough house, but that is another case of camouflage.

name: Glidden, Norman Frank
motto: It's better to be a live lobster than a dead social lion
description: It was rumored that "Nick" was P. T. Barnum's original wild man. At any rate he came to us on crutches. In a few months at N. H. C. "Nick" had developed such a high state of civilization that he cast away his crutches and now walks upright like any man. We came near losing a valuable (?) man when, last summer, "Nick" jumped into the brook "so's to cool off." Although his appearances are against him, "Nick" has a way of showing up the most of us when it comes to the final mark.

name: Greenfield, Sara Ella
motto: To those who know thee not, no words can paint; And those who know thee, know all words are faint.
description: Sara comes to us from Rochester and the question arises as to whether she is really a resident or a commuter as nearly every week-end finds her at home. Sara's college activities are varied and numerous ranging from membership in the Y. W. C. A. cabinet to playing her violin in the girls' orchestra, or displaying her artistic skill with her paints and paint brush. Whenever any one wants something done it is "Let Sara do it" and Sara does it, too. Ready for fun or ready for work, Sara will never be one to shirk. The one charge we hold against her is the lack of affection in her freshman year. Although "he" did his best "Sara didn't reciprocate."

name: Hale, Dorothy Adaline
motto: She claims the whole and not the part; The coin of an unused heart.
description: Demure and sweet Dorothy entered college and continued quietly on her way, her freshman year, never becoming excited over anything. But her sophomore year was her downfall. Her interest and enthusiasm began to grow and her friends soon found that to her Botany 51 class was a "Boomer." It would almost seem that Dorothy has neglected her studies this year in order to make sweaters and wristers, and to write letters to a man in khaki.

name: Hall, Irene Marguerite
motto: Irene's only failing is an incurable liking for courses in Horticulture.
description: In fact the annual horticultural exhibits wouldn't be complete without some of her assistance. A good student nearly all of the time and a good sport all of the time, Irene is one of those that are always willing to lend a hand either in work or in fun.

name: Hanson, Dorothy
motto: It is better to be little and shine, Than to be large and cast a shadow.
description: Small in stature but not in ideas. Her two ambitions are, to grow, and to benefit the human race by practical knowledge derived from taking Billie Groves' sociology courses. When Dorothy was a freshman she was a most credulous child, and took all things for truth. But now she thinks for herself. She takes all the hard courses in the catalogue, and is wearing laurels in sociology for her brilliant thesis. She purses up her lips and spiels on and on about theory and practice. To Dorothy everything is exciting, from an ice cream to Grand Opera in Boston.

name: Harvell, Ralph Burbank
motto: Harve's athletic ability was clearly in evidence, especially during his first year, as he was awarded an 'N. H.' in football and track. His activities were not confined to athletics however as his winning ways won someone who meant more to him, than did winning football games. It would seem that the bell in 'T' hall had taken the place of the proverbial fiddle, in winning the heart of a fair co-ed. Still considering all things, he is a 'hide' of a good fellow.

name: Hilliard, William Russell
motto: A plow-man on his leg is higher than a gentleman on his knee.
description: "Russ" came to us from the classical shades of Sanborn Seminary. After commuting for two years he decided to devote the remainder of his career as a student in the wilds of Durham. When the war broke out "Russ" decided to drop the plow, and study aviation. We're afraid if he ever reaches the unknown heights he will be taken into Heaven for an angel. When "Russ" becomes proficient in the art of washing "Pa" Taylor's Ford, he will be a full fledged Alpha Zeta.

name: Hoffman, Louis Benjamin
description: Louis is a good student and an excellent violinist but in spite of this he is very modest. Louis must have left his heart in Manchester for during his entire freshman year he remained unmoved by the smiles of the co-eds. However the "inevitable" came last year and Louis fell. Although registered as an engineer he appears to have a hearty interest in the economics department. Louis has become so proficient in the manipulation of "Polly"-nomials that he is now taking advanced math. courses under Steck.

name: Hunt, Cyril Thomas
motto: "You may lead an ass to knowledge, But you cannot make him think"
description: "Cy" rained down from an unknown region with an unknown name and undertook to become a Chem-icker. However, "Cy" caught the flying bug and the odor of the chem. building held no more attraction for him. "Cy" is a member in good standing of the Amalgamated Dish Washers' Union, since becoming the Kitchen Workers of the World. He was once a good type of common crustacean known as the crab but continued association with real men converted him into a less dangerous variety of bull-throwers.

name: Joy, Frank Adin
motto: "The grinders may cease—but the grind goes on forever"
description: This class grind hails from Newfields. Although his name is Joy, he is always in fear that the mark on his next prelim will not be A. As a satellite of Professor Porter, Frank shows some brilliancy.

name: Kelleher, Mary Ethel
motto: Music was in the soul of her, And in that was her delight
description: Most of us envy Ethel the advantage of living at home in the nearby city, but we do not covet the early rising thereby entailed. With the advent of a small sister she resolved to restrict her social life in order to set a good example; but the idea didn't work out—and she's still a matter of necessity at every good time. In all honesty we must admit Ethel is a member of the Girls' Glee Club by virtue of her fingers rather than of her voice. She is ever ready to give pleasure to her friends by playing, and her contribution to many a Book and Scroll meeting has been the most enjoyable part of the program.

name: Kemp, Alice B.
motto: Then will I count this loving toil well spent
description: Quiet? Yes, but underneath that calm exterior lurks a fund of tireless energy and firmness of purpose that stands her in good stead whether she is playing basketball or serving as a Y. W. C. A. delegate. Alice is an indefatigable worker in college organizations while her loyalty to her Alma Mater governs her every thought and act. In fact, it is said that the responsibility she feels for upholding the reputation of N. H. C. rests heavily upon her shoulders on glee club trips. Never mind, Alice, with your unswerving fidelity and cool judgment, you are a friend worth having, and what better could be said?

name: Langley, Ada Caroline
motto: Thou speak'st aright; I am that merry wanderer of the night
description: Verily I say unto you, discard not thy Ford, for the corporation predicts a scarcity through the continuance of the war. It is well known that Fords are one of the best sausage carriers on the market. Where a dance is being planned Ada simply must go, regardless of Mother's "no," and stiff knees. Ada and her family are a familiar sight, driving into town early in the morning. Commuters in the rest room first thought the Germans were invading until they became accustomed to her delicate tread in "T" hall corridor.

name: Lewis, Marion Anne
motto: As sweet as she is pretty
clubs: Secretary of the Y. W. C. A. (2)
Vice-President of Y. W. C. A. (3)
Delegate to Vocational Conference at Wheaton College (2)
description: From the wilds of Littleton, Marion came to us with two worries, her marks and her increasing corpulency. The first need cause her no worry, but the second is still unsolved for each year she thinks she gains a few more pounds. Her reputation as a soloist is well known and she is in demand on all musical occasions. Her outside work keeps her busy for she enters every activity with the same zeal she puts in her studies. She takes no more walks along the Madbury road but all paths for her lead to the post office where she receives news of the CROSS.

name: McCarty, Mary Elizabeth Anna
motto: Perseverance gains its need, And patience wins the race
description: Mary has been known to have "visions" in English 55 until she visited a "doctor from the Portsmouth Navy Yard." But visions have not hindered Mary from doing her bit for she has knit wristers for her Plattsburg friend who tutored her in French this summer. She has never paid any attention to the opposite sex while in college until this year when she was badly smitten by a senior.

name: Matthes, Carl Fredrich
motto: Knowledge is Power—if you know it about the right person
description: "Cat" came to us from the confines of Methuen as confirmed an Epworth Leaguer as could be desired. It took him two years to grow his social wings, but now you can't see him for dust. He has even been known to carry a cocktail bottle on Y. M. C. A. deputation trips. Trenches have offered no difficulty to him but the barb-wire entanglements about the telephone office proved too much for him one night. "Cat's" worst faults are spending his Lab. periods in Dover and sleeping under the spell of Olus's soothing voice. Richmond is the capital of Virginia but "Bud" doesn't stand for Budweiser.

name: Moody, Alden Howard
motto: Wild oats make a bad autumn crop
description: When "Shrimp" was a freshman he was what might be termed a model boy. We little thought that this youngster who blushed like the proverbial school-girl when he saw a co-ed, would become a ladies' man. He annexed an affinity from the capitol city, showed her the way to the registrar's office, and though a chem. man we think he will turn out to be an entomologist, because he is always thinking, where'll Bugbee.

name: Nightingale, Gordon Thayer
motto: He who fights and runs away, Will live to write about the fray
clubs: National Junior A. A. A. U. Cross Country Champion 1917
description: Gordon can give any of them a good running fight, and the best part of it is that he gets away with it. In fact he seems to be one of the original speed boys. It is rumored that while running down the pine blister rust last summer, Gordon met someone that he didn't try to run away from. At any rate we are ready to bet that it was a good one that ever caught G. T. Nightingale.

name: Nudd, Willard Eugene
motto: Diligence is the mother of good luck
clubs: Advertising Manager of the Engineering Club
description: Willie Nudd, expert motorcyclist, mechanical engineer and student, is an individual of mystery and solitude. Owing to the present shortage of coal "Bill" is busily engaged in designing a boiler which will derive its heat from hot air furnished from Finky's unlimited supply.

name: Owen, Wilfrid Lester
motto: 'Tis better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all.
description: "Lefty" landed here from God's country with the intention of taking the "Aggie" course. He didn't realize his capabilities because now he is taking "King" James' course and specializing in "Tobogganning 51." However, he will fulfill the Eleventh Commandment of the Engineer if it takes an extra year. We wonder who his "latest" is.

name: Page, Oral Allen
motto: He who owes nothing fears nothing.
description: The Co-eds have all decided that our rosy-cheeked Cupid from "up Kingston way" is the prettiest boy in the class and there have been several unsuccessful attempts to drag him into society by the "light fantastic route." "Pete's" chief trials are trying to get "Tommie" out of bed in time for eight o'clocks and refraining from commenting on Botany 55. If you want to see him blush, just ask him about those pink letters postmarked East Kingston. "Hey, fellows! It's seven o'clock. Time to get up."

name: Perkins, Caroline May
motto: A good laugh is sunshine in a house.
description: To the casual observer, her college life seems to consist of busy dashes across the campus to basketball practice and Glee Club rehearsals, where she shoots baskets or warbles ditties with vigorous cheerfulness. In fact, vigor is the thing which characterizes her, as anyone may testify who heard her in the performance of "Midsummer Night's Dream," during which the audience sat spell-bound, wondering how such manly roars could emerge from the lungs of any mortal maid. But underneath all this is a rare seriousness of purpose which makes her a deep thinker as well as a quick doer. The world needs more people like Caroline.

name: Pinkham, Madeline Lona
motto: She liked whate'er she looked on, and her looks went everywhere
description: Is it her ability to attract or her winning brown eyes that makes her an asset to our social life? She doesn't have to worry about a bid to the next dance for her friends are many and far from slow; neither is Madeline slow when it comes to chemistry. The tax on stamps has not decreased Madeline's correspondence, which began with the war, and will end—She is taking several library courses, mostly in social conversation.

name: Place, Palmer Bruce
motto: Gee! Whizz!
description: "Placie" was drafted into the "King's" army of chemical reprobates when he first entered the portals of New Hampshire and has not, as yet been misled into the "Arts and Crafts" division for lazy people. If you want a good time show him a drum and watch him go. He is also a pianist. No, that's wrong; it should have been, "He also plays the piano." He sometimes smokes but never after eating.

name: Poland, Mary Flora
motto: You love me little if you can't stand my song
description: Ever since Mary came to New Hampshire she has been known for her desire to be a rival of Melba's and a second Paderewski and night and day mournful sounds are heard issuing from her lonely attic. Although she did not make the Glee Club, there are rumors that she may be admitted to that exclusive society "Les Petits Oiseaux." Her reputation as an athlete has spread even to Hanover. She is foremost in all social activities and the question always asked is, "Which one is it tonight, Mary?" In spite of her popularity she still eagerly awaits the mail from "Somewhere in France."

name: Prescott, Frank Williams
description: "Kaloma" came from a little dump called Pittsfield, but he won't admit that it is a little dump and exploits at length on the achievements of his own home town. He does a thriving laundry business and at one time attempted to introduce a fish industry. However, fish and hard-boiled shirts didn't harmonize, so he turned the fast-waning enterprise over to his roommate and co—or counter—bullthrower, Clapp. His music leads him to higher art and his bullthrowing to a lower Art (Clapp). But give him his twenty-year-old pipe and one of Scottie's history text books and he will sit still for hours, if tied with strong rope.

name: Randall, Christine
motto: Modest, simple, and sweet, the very type of Priscilla
description: Christine always gives the impression of accomplishing every task without the least bit of hustle and bustle and worry. Things just seem to do themselves when she is around. There is no disorder and confusion whatsoever. Only one event is able to bring a little flush of "joy" to her cheeks, and that is the arrival of the weekly letter with the inscription "passed by censor." She is surely the most true and loyal of friends and to know her at all is to wish to know her better. Christine has even been able to find that there is some "joy" in math.

name: Richmond, Louise Mary
motto: The personification of optimism
description: Yes, she did!—at last she succumbed to pressure and gave up commuting. She says it was to save her people from starting the day so early—but we can't believe it. "Bud" talks sines and cosines in her sleep and usually has a Calculus tucked under one arm. At unearthly hours, after an Informal, or in the gray dawn of a winter's day, she may be found audibly pondering over the possibility of making the sub-tangent twine around the normal. She draws a princely salary by assisting the Extension Department in Morrill Hall; and she spends the money for a mixture of Liberty Bonds and jelly doughnuts.

name: Robb, Bernice Aurilla
motto: The way to a man's heart is through his stomach
description: Bernice is surely efficient in the art of cooking and although perhaps too generous to suit Hoover, is not too much so to suit those who are favored with her delicious pies and cakes. Maybe this explains why there is more than one caller at her home on Wednesday evenings. Probably her abundant knowledge acquired in Chemistry 68 under the King has helped her even though Bernice took this course just to show us she could shark Chemistry if she liked the instructor. We are sorry Bernice cannot stay at the Dorm. so more of the girls could appreciate her good nature and jolly stories of what they used to do back home.

name: Rogers, Walter Eugene
motto: If I only had two good legs I could step some
description: No doubt Zene will make a good aviator as he had plenty of practice at the highflying game when he was in college. Perhaps he is strong enough to stay in the army, but he sure was sickly while in college. His best excuse for cutting a class was acute indigestion. Professors will testify that this wasn't his only ailment as he has had everything from a cold to pneumonia, length of recovery depending on when he wanted to attend the next class. His automobiles have relieved him from government inspection for the last two years. Too bad he can't have one with him now.

name: Rumrill, Hamilton
motto: O! Sleep, it is a gentle thing
description: Rummy belongs to the latter class and has even become so proficient as to hold down a position on the Somersworth High faculty. Report has it that one of the girls in the school has fallen in love with him. Really he is a good proposition for any woman. He's as sound as a dollar, will stand without hitching and has musical ability which gains fame and renown wherever it is heard.

name: Sanders, Miriam Augusta
motto:
Light was her hair as the fairy flax,
Her cheek like the dawn of day
description: Miriam has broken a maximum number of rules in the last three years, but she is nevertheless extracting much of value from her college course. She has bestowed her favor in turn upon the basketball team, the Hikers' Club, and last upon somewhat extended "instruction" trips to Boston—but she's not fickle—merely versatile—and she's still very young. We all look up to Miriam (we have to, some of us as much as eight inches); and we expect to look still higher before the war is over. Ask her to sing for you the song which may win her a bid to "Les Petites Oiseaux."

name: Seawards, Susie Ethel
motto: Courteous though coy, and gentle though retired.
description: Sue commuted from Dover for the first two years, where if rumors are true she had wonderful times, especially Sunday after church! This year Sue stays in Durham for Dover has lost all its attraction for her. Sue is a good sport and is always ready for a good time. Those brown eyes are always full of mischief.

name: Shedd, Hazelle Maud
motto: Actresses will happen in the best regulated families.
description: No one knows just what would have happened in our study of bugs if Hazelle hadn't decided that the "Professor" wasn't clever enough to sit on the whole class. In fact she is so wide awake that nothing escapes her eyes. Some wonder why Hazelle so often visits the faculty mail box, but the well informed know that those letters from Texas are frequent and most interesting. Anyhow we may all be assured that her extra experience in cooking will not go amiss.

name: Shuttleworth, Melba Johnson
motto: For if she will, she will, and you may depend on't. And if she won't she won't, and there's an end on't.
clubs: Delegate to the Y. W. C. A. Conference at Silver Bay
description: Melba has always been popular at informals and at all college activities. This year her interest is centered in a nearby seashore town and in long distance calls at exactly nine o'clock every night. She is especially fond of Geology 51.

name: Shuttleworth, William Edward
motto: We met—We gazed—I saw and sighed—Byron
description: From the home of Frank Jones and the Sunset League Bill came to New Hampshire College. During his first year's stay in the dorm. Pa Curtis became grayheaded, but now that Bill has found his place in athletics he is using his pep to better advantage. The only worry he has in life is whether the next mail train from Lynn is on time. If you want to know his record as a duck-hunter ask Sid Perley. Theoretically speaking corn is king but we do not think that any plantation that Bill may own will be complete without Cotton.

name: Shillaber, John James
motto: Tell the truth and shame the—family
description: Jack's one ambition to become a full-fledged engineer is still possible. He strengthened his chances along this line by bringing his dog from Portsmouth to attend classes for him. The great interest shown by him for financial affairs in his freshman year was finally rewarded by his being elected class treasurer. His fondest hope is that a course in the care and operation of automobiles will be established and he will be allowed to instruct it. In fact he likes the town so well that he spends most of his vacations here.

name: Smith, Gertrude May
motto: Yes in her garden grow Far more than herbs and flowers, Kind thoughts, contentment, peace of mind And joy for weary hours.
description: In her freshman days Gertrude commuted but the trains were so often late that she decided to stay in town in order to get to classes on time. Prompted by her never failing good nature and sympathy Gertrude works her way into the midst of us.

name: Smith, Mabel Foster
motto: The minstrel boy to war is gone—
description: At least, Mabel has every assurance that her "minstrel boy" will come back "Victor," whether or no he "brings her a Turk and Kaiser too." Meanwhile, she is calmly awaiting his return, pursuing the even tenor of her way as serenely as though no thrilling romance had ever entered her young life. In fact, she intends to graduate with us if she gets by Chem. There's just one difference, however,—we'll wager Mabel isn't knitting for the Red Cross. Here's to you, Mabel! May we all face the crisis as sanely and bravely as you are doing.

name: Stafford, John Fremont
motto: The ways of the transgressor are smooth
description: The pride of Berlin, "Johnie" started out as a would-be engineer, and under the guidance of Duke Sawyer lasted one whole year, but when Duke went, John lost courage. "Dynamite" is now taking a course which might be called hash; it is a combination of oratory under Lefty and accounting under Conda, and from the way John talks when his books don't balance, one would conclude that he was a born orator. At stacking beds and raising rough-house he has no equal, having held the championship of the third floor at Fairchild Hall his freshman year.

name: Strain, Murry Hartshorn
motto: The self-made man is often proud of a poor job
description: This abbreviated young gent descended from the snow capped mountains in the fall of 1915 and lived a life of seclusion at the country club. In this sanctum of peace and quiet, he cherished notions of future fussing parties, and now it is only a bum schedule and rotten train connections that prevent him from living in Dover.

name: Sutherland, Christine Jane
motto: Happy is she who wears a diamond ring
description: When Tena was in high school she said she wanted to be a chemistry teacher, but her college chemistry was so discouraging, she decided that the next best thing would be to marry a chemistry professor. Now, housekeeping is quite an art, and for a good foundation in learning, there is nothing like a course at Smith Hall. Ask Tena. Christine is always ready for a good time and yet can see the serious side of things. She is always popular among her schoolmates and has faithfully served three years as class secretary.

name: Thompson, William Haven Jr.
motto: All the witchery of Ireland was in her eyes
description: "Tommie" drifted up from "New York" way, having decided, after four years in the cold, cruel world, that his education wasn't quite complete. Someone kidded him into believing that he could dance and now you can't keep him away from the music. He spent his freshman year reading Omar and Kipling, but now that he is the manager of an orchestra, he has cut out the solo stuff. Speaking of mathematics, "Tommie" says that, at a cent a word, it would take a champagne income to express his opinion of the subject. His chief ambitions are to find out what it feels like to be drunk and to use the "King's" beaker at keg parties.

name: Tilton, Louis Blake
motto: Sleep on, beloved, sleep and take thy rest
description: "Tilt" being a "somnambulist" walked into Durham one stormy evening and before waking up registered in the Agri. course at N. H. C. His chief object in life is to arrange his schedule so that he can spend Saturday in the slaughter of the wild beasts of East Kingston. While asleep on his motorcycle one spring afternoon he fell off and lost a major portion of his face in the mud of Durham. This sad catastrophe has left "Tilt" in a state of despair.

name: Torrey, Prescott Hawes
motto: I dunno but you're right
description: From the wilds of Florida via Quincy and Derry came this Gopher hunter. We almost believed his stories of snakes, gophers and duck hunting until his brother who was with him in Florida came to "New Hampshire." "Pres's" favorite amusements seem to be ski jumping, gopher hunting and telling exaggerated stories of his varied experiences in the sunny south. It is hard to believe that a woman could affect such a strong mind.

name: Wakefield, Clement Arthur
motto: Angelface
description: Some say that "Clem" is the biggest "kidder" in college. In fact he admits it. It is his personality and winning ways that makes many a trip to nearby centers of social activities successful. We shudder to think what would happen if "Angelface" should fail to receive that daily violet tinted epistle from sa chere amie via Montreal. His activities, which consist of managing the basketball team and consoling widows in the absence of their loved ones, are proficiently performed. "Clem" started to be an Engineer, but now has changed to an Artful Grafter specializing in library courses and the distribution of contagious disease germs.

name: Wetherbee, Emma Louise
motto: Better late than never
description: This may be Emma's motto in class work but not so in correspondence. That is regular every day. Whenever going to Dover, one knows where to find company for a good time. Emma has won many friends by her pleasant disposition.

name: Wheeler, William Crawford
motto: Only the young die good
clubs: Assistant Business Manager The New Hampshire (3)
description: Bill isn't dead yet. He blew into town as unassuming and inconspicuous a youngster as the North Country ever produced. Athletic aspirations got hold of "Bill" and he fetched up as official rubber for the real athletes. Long and close association with this class of men has caused protuberances to sprout in his cervical region, but as he can still wear a stiff collar with some degree of comfort, we stand ready to pronounce him safe.

name: White, Alpheus Britton
motto: For we are only poor weak mortals after all
description: The next time that "Koon" shakes hands with you after vacation or any other furlough, kick him on the knees and plaster up that bewitching smile. He'll say he's glad to see you but he tells them all the same thing. Don't swallow it all and think you're some guy. He spreads it the same over every man's door. The "Koon" has two perplexities; first, that trains from his town cannot penetrate the midwinter snow drifts, and second, that he has not received a military sweater, except from his aunt.

name: Wiggin, Ida Marion
motto: Her eyes are like the heaven's own blue
description: If you have ever heard the latest rag time played in a most bewitching manner in "T" Hall or Smith Hall you will have fallen to Ida's musical charms. Ida says there are advantages in being a commuter for callers may be received any time and we have been told that they were rather numerous last year. Plank steaks are very appetizing, but it is a decided fact that Ida prefers a "Chuck" (not designating the kind.)

name: Wildes, Karl Leland
motto: How hath the mighty fallen
clubs: Assistant Business Manager of the GRANITE
description: As president of the Crescent R. H. A., Karl proved to be a big success during his freshman year. He started out on the honor list but yielded to the call of the "Wilde" woman of Newmarket. This first class pessimist opened the door one night and fell in the draft, being placed in class 1A. One of the many things which Karl has done for his Alma Mater and which all English students should appreciate is that he kept Doctor Richards from freezing to death last winter. After a short course in boiler design this young man came to the conclusion that a boiler without water is like a "Hobo without food."

name: Williams, Katharine
motto: A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance
description: "Katy" is always in for a good time as well as keeping up with her scholarship. She commuted two years but this year is a "Ballardite" and is surely fascinated with dormitory life. For a while "Katy" was rather partial to "Sammies" but now a certain "Ladd" seems to be favored. Her greatest pleasure is in house parties during the summer but then, one wouldn't tell anything on "Katy."

name: Young, Roland Jesse
motto: Now I think you'll find,
description: Brigham came down from Berlin with the rest of the Berlin roughnecks who invaded the third floor of the dorm, and whom "Pa" Curtis says cannot be beat. His favorite pastime is reading books of this type: "After Death What?", "Letters from a Living Dead Man", "Cosmic Consciousness", etc. Ask him about one of these and he will say, "I know, but just can't explain." His hobby was the aeroplane and today you will find him at the aviation school in Texas preparing to fly.
